title: MLS client not reset on reconnect: stale group state accumulates
description: What is awkward: On Reconnecting, commands.rs sets mls_active=false and online.clear() (lines 124-125) but does not reset the MlsClient. The mls variable retains its old group state (mls.group is Some). When the admin reconnects and create_group() is called, MlsGroup::new_with_group_id creates a new group using the same in-memory provider that has the old group's epoch keys. The old state is never garbage-collected and the in-memory provider accumulates orphaned key material. For non-admins, mls.group contains the old group but mls_active=false, so process_incoming is not called -- the old state just sits there. Suggested rewrite: on Reconnecting, do mls = MlsClient::new(&my_pub_id)?; to get a completely fresh MLS client. This is correct because MLS state is documented as ephemeral (mls.rs header comment: 'Group state is ephemeral... Each connect session starts fresh').
notes: CONFIRMED from openmls builder.rs:87-92: build_internal checks MlsGroup::load(provider.storage(), &group_id).is_some() with replace_old_group=false (the default). If the group exists, it returns Err(NewGroupError::GroupAlreadyExists). On admin reconnect, create_group() fails with this error, mls_active stays false, and mls.group retains the old session's group object. This is the direct root cause of nie-7xs.2. The P0 severity of nie-7xs.2 belongs here -- fixing this (reset mls on Reconnecting) would also fix nie-7xs.2.
